model_tasks: customer service representative (csr) sends a mortgage offer
csr waits for a customer reply
csr  generates request for outstanding documents

additional_tasks: csr  generates request for outstanding documents

merged_tasks.model: customer service representative (csr) sends a mortgage offer
csr waits for a customer reply

user_text:
The process begins when a Customer Service Representative (CSR) sends a mortgage offer to a customer. After sending the offer, the CSR waits for a reply from the customer. Once a reply is received, the process reaches a decision point where two possible paths can be taken.

In the first path, the CSR updates the case details. Following this update, the CSR archives the work. After archiving, the CSR performs a cancellation. This path then leads to another decision point, which eventually merges back into the main flow of the process.

In the second path, the CSR moves the case to administration. This leads to another decision point. From here, the CSR may generate a request for any outstanding documents needed. After generating this request, the process reaches yet another decision point. If the process continues along this path, the CSR updates the case details, archives the work, and performs a cancellation, similar to the first path. This sequence also leads to a decision point that merges back into the main flow.

Both paths eventually converge at a final decision point, which leads to the end of the process. The process concludes once all necessary actions have been completed and the final decision point is reached.
output_text:
The process begins when a Customer Service Representative (CSR) sends a mortgage offer to a customer. After sending the offer, the CSR waits for a reply from the customer. Once a reply is received, a decision point is reached where the CSR must choose between two paths.

In the first path, the CSR updates the case details. Following this, the CSR archives the work and then performs a cancellation. After the cancellation, another decision point is encountered, but regardless of the decision made here, the process continues to the next decision point.

In the second path, the CSR moves the case to administration. This leads to another decision point. If the decision is to generate a request for outstanding documents, the CSR does so and then updates the case details. After updating the case details, the CSR archives the work and performs a cancellation. Following the cancellation, a decision point is reached, but regardless of the decision made, the process continues to the next decision point.

Both paths eventually converge at a common decision point, and from there, the process concludes.
